samples.query: rental car size allowed
reference_contexts:
# **What size rental car am I allowed to book?**

Book up to intermediate size vehicles (mini, economy, or compact) through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Refer to section 6.1 Rental Car [Vehicle Size](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#6) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Electric and hybrid vehicles**

Amazon encourages the use of electric or hybrid rental vehicles where available. If the only electric or hybrid vehicles available are standard or full-size, you may book these instead of an intermediate size vehicle.

## **Exceptions for larger vehicles**

Standard, full-size cars, intermediate SUVs, or vans are permitted in the following situations:

- Three (3) or more travelers traveling together and sharing the rental vehicle
- Large work equipment requiring additional cargo space
- Pre-approved medical reasons
- Safety exceptions (for example, AWD/4WD in winter in locations prone to blizzards)

## **Safe driving requirements**

Consider the risks associated with driving, especially outside your home country and after long flights.

### **Driver requirements**

- Hold a driving license suitable for the type of car rented and permitted by local law.
- Be familiar with local driving conditions before renting.

### **Risk management**

- Car rental is not advised unless you are familiar with local conditions.
- Follow [Travel Risk Management](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelRiskManagement/Home/) (TRM) ground transportation recommendations for elevated risk destinations in TRM advisories.
samples.reference_answer: You can book up to intermediate size vehicles (mini, economy, or compact) through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool.

samples.query: When I'm changing a flight in the self-service tool, what's the actual sequence to cancel one versus just modifying a segment?
reference_contexts:
# **I need to change my travel plans**

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to cancel or rebook your trip. Self-service supports cancellations and modifications for most bookings.

Refer to section 3.10 [Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **To cancel a flight**

- Log in to the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).
- Navigate to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip.
- Select Cancel next to the flight.
- Confirm the cancellation twice to complete.

## **To modify a flight**

- Select your segment and select Modify.
- Choose the flight to change.
- Update your details.
- Search for new flights and complete checkout.

Watch this quick video: [Modify Your Flight & Hotel](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1594156).
samples.reference_answer: To cancel a flight: log in to the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool, go to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip, select Cancel next to the flight, then confirm the cancellation twice to complete. To modify a flight: select your segment and choose Modify, pick the flight to change, update your details, then search for new flights and complete checkout.

samples.query: out of policy vs policy violation difference approval
reference_contexts:
# **Why is my booking out of policy?**

The "Out of Policy" flag in the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) helps you identify options that comply with the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#1). It does not prevent you from completing your booking.

## **What the Global Travel and Expense Policy considers**

- Lowest Logical Fare in economy with a seat assignment

- Lowest hotel rates

- Preferred car rates for the approved car type

## **Out of Policy vs. Policy Violation**

Out of Policy: Your selection exceeds policy guidelines but may be justified for business reasons

Yes — provide a reason for your selection.

Policy Violation: Your selection violates a hard rule (for example, booking first class). No — the booking cannot be completed.

## **When an out-of-policy booking is justified**

You may proceed with an out-of-policy booking when there is a valid business reason. Examples include:

- A hotel that is out of policy but closer to your business meetings

- An intermediate SUV during inclement weather for safety reasons

Provide a brief reason when prompted. This serves as an audit tool to ensure flexibility while maintaining safety and frugality.
samples.reference_answer: Out of Policy means your selection exceeds policy guidelines but may be justified for business reasons; you can complete the booking by providing a reason. Policy Violation means your selection breaks a hard rule (for example, booking first class), and the booking cannot be completed.

samples.query: If I'm travelling in EMEA or APAC and the fare with a standard seat or baggage ends up costing more than the Lowest Logical Fare limit, how should I handle that from an expense standpoint?
reference_contexts:
# **What is the process for booking a seat?**

Book the Lowest Logical Fare with a seat assignment and baggage included. The [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) highlights in-policy fares for easy selection.

Refer to section 4.7 [Air Travel Reimbursable Incidentals](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **EMEA and APAC travelers: ancillary purchases**

Fares that include a standard seat or baggage may sometimes exceed the Lowest Logical Fare limits in EMEA and APAC regions. If this occurs:

- Purchase the seat or baggage separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline.
- Expense the additional cost separately.
- Save all receipts for any ancillary purchases.

## **How to select a seat when included in your fare**

Seat maps are available during the shopping path only. Use them to compare seat availability across flights before making your selection.

### **During booking**

A seat may be auto-assigned when you select a flight at checkout. You may be able to select a different seat — view the seat map at checkout and choose your preferred seat before completing your booking.

### **After booking**

Change your seat once ticketed by:

- Navigating to Trips > My Trips in the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool
- Using the airline mobile app directly (varies by airline)

Watch this quick video: [How to Change Your Seat](https://broadcast.amazon.com/videos/1656533)
samples.reference_answer: For EMEA and APAC travelers, if a fare including a standard seat or baggage exceeds the Lowest Logical Fare limits, you should purchase the seat or baggage separately as an ancillary item directly with the airline, expense the additional cost separately, and save all receipts for any ancillary purchases.

# **How do I request medical accommodation for travel?**

Contact [Disability and Leave Services](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/AET/Reyes-org/global-Travel/) (DLS) to request travel accommodations due to a physical or disability-related impairment or limitation.

Refer to section 3.5 [Travelers with Additional Needs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dac58a98-3508-3b21-6d99-2fb50bd5db01) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Who authorizes travel accommodations?**

Only DLS can authorize travel accommodations. Amazon Travel & Events and your manager cannot authorize accommodation requests.

## **How to request**

samples.query: If I want to upgrade my fare class on a regional trip, how does the reimbursement work and what costs do I have to cover myself?
reference_contexts:
# **Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class?** (Regional)

Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket.

Refer to section 4.1[Airline Cabin Class](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **How to upgrade**

- Contact a Travel Counselor and request a personal upgrade for your trip.
- Pay for the entire ticket cost upfront using your own payment method.
- Obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and the upgraded class.
- When you expense your trip, claim reimbursement only for the documented Lowest Logical Fare noted by the travel services provider.

Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are not reimbursable.

## **Manager notification**

Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.
samples.reference_answer: You're allowed to upgrade, but Amazon only reimburses the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class, so you must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket. To do this, contact a Travel Counselor to request a personal upgrade, pay the entire ticket cost upfront with your own payment method, and obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and the upgraded class. When expensing the trip, claim reimbursement only for the documented Lowest Logical Fare noted by the travel services provider. Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are not reimbursable.

reference_contexts:
# **Why do I have to book travel in the program?**

Booking within [Amazon Travel & Events](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/) is required. The program provides safety, cost savings, booking simplicity, sustainability, and service quality benefits that are not available on consumer booking sites.

## **Benefits of booking within Amazon Travel & Events**

- Safety and security: Amazon Travel & Events provide 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details for quick assistance. All bookings are automatically registered for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.
- Cost savings: Amazon Travel & Events negotiates exclusive discounts with preferred suppliers. You access corporate rates on 400+ airlines, millions of hotel rooms, and car rental agencies that are not available on consumer booking sites.
- Booking simplicity: Access over 400 airlines and millions of hotel rooms through pre-vetted travel suppliers who meet rigorous safety and service standards.
- Sustainability: In line with Amazon's commitment to The Climate Pledge, purposeful travel options such as lower-emission rail and eco-conscious hotels are available. Select sustainable travel options when available.
- Service quality: Pre-screened travel providers meet high standards for reliability and customer care.

## **Before you book**

Review section 3.0 [General Booking Guidelines](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4ec58a98-347a-e36a-54e0-278512f5781b) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy before booking.
samples.reference_answer: Amazon Travel & Events provides 24/7 emergency support with instant access to your travel details for quick assistance. All bookings are automatically registered for security monitoring and medical assistance worldwide through International SOS.

# **How do I set up my Travel Profile?**

You must create a Travel Profile in the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) before booking business travel. Your Travel Profile securely stores your personal information, passport details, payment method, and travel preferences.

Refer to section 3.4 [Traveler Profiles](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#52c58a98-3509-0d43-4f83-20fcd24c2fec) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **What your Travel Profile stores**

- Personal information: mobile phone number and date of birth (varies by country)

- Passport details (for international travel)

- Corporate or personal credit card (required to secure hotel reservations)

- Travel preferences and loyalty program numbers (optional)

## **Quick start videos**

## **How to book after profile setup**

- Standard bookings: Log in to the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and search for flights, hotels, car rentals, or rail options.
- Last-minute bookings (within 6 hours of departure): Contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) during business hours. For same-day travel outside the 6-hour window, book online through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

samples.query: premium economy upgrade eligibility long haul flights
reference_contexts:
# **Can I get permission to upgrade for long-haul flights?**

Yes. Amazon has a defined process for granting cabin upgrade exceptions through the Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program. Travelers who meet specific criteria are approved to self-book premium economy for flights of 14 hours or more (excluding layovers).

## **Eligibility requirements**

To qualify for PEA, you must meet both criteria:

- Book all travel through Amazon Travel & Events.
- Complete four (4) or more one-way trips exceeding 14 hours (excluding layover time) in the last 12 months.

## **How PEA selection works**

No action is required from you. Once you fulfill the minimum requirements, your name is automatically added to the PEA list.

- Your [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) is automatically updated to allow booking premium economy flights.
- Qualifications are monitored daily.
- Once minimum criteria are fulfilled, you maintain status for one year from the qualifying date.
- No manual review or employee action is required.

## **Checking your PEA status**

Open a request through [Travel RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=GTE&q0=8110c0c9-104e-4744-85d6-041cbc64db91) to verify your PEA status. Amazon Travel Support can verify status but cannot provide exceptions.

## **What PEA does not cover**

PEA exceptions do not apply to all flights. The exception applies only to flights of 14 hours or more (excluding layovers).
samples.reference_answer: To qualify for the Premium Economy Accommodation (PEA) program, you must meet both criteria: book all travel through Amazon Travel & Events, and complete four (4) or more one-way trips exceeding 14 hours (excluding layover time) in the last 12 months. PEA applies only to flights of 14 hours or more (excluding layovers).

samples.query: If I want to use my personal miles to upgrade a flight I booked for work, how does that process work and are there any cost or eligibility limits I should watch out for?
reference_contexts:
# **Can I upgrade my fare using miles?**

Yes. After booking travel through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/), you may contact the airline directly to request an upgrade using your personal loyalty miles. Mileage upgrades cannot be processed through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) — you must request them directly with your airline.

Refer to section 3.12 [Loyalty Programs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#c6c58a98-350b-aaa2-8c87-d9cb7f980da2) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Important limitations**

- Airline policies vary: Not all airlines allow upgrades with miles for tickets purchased through third-party booking platforms.
- Fare class restrictions: Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically ineligible for mileage upgrades.
- Verification required: Review your specific fare rules and restrictions before requesting an upgrade.
samples.reference_answer: Yes, you can upgrade using your personal loyalty miles, but you must contact the airline directly after booking through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool — mileage upgrades cannot be processed through the tool itself. Be aware of limitations: not all airlines allow mileage upgrades for tickets purchased through third-party booking platforms, basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically ineligible, and you should review your specific fare rules and restrictions before requesting an upgrade. See section 3.12 Loyalty Programs in the Global Travel and Expense Policy for details.

# **Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?**

Yes. You can earn personal loyalty miles and points when traveling for business through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). This applies to airlines, hotels, and rental cars.

Refer to section 3.12 [Loyalty Programs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-12) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Eligibility restrictions**

Employees based in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

## **How to earn miles**

- Locate more information on the Amazon Travel & Events [Preferred Travel Providers and Promotions Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/BusinessTravelPromotions/).
- Sign up for loyalty programs.
- Add your loyalty program numbers to your Travel Profile.
- Miles and rewards are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip.

## **Important requirements**

- Ensure your loyalty numbers are accurate in your Travel Profile to avoid missing credits.
- Your Travel Profile name and your loyalty program name must match exactly.
- Contact the travel provider directly if miles are not credited after your trip.

Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket.

- On the Checkout page, review the breakdown of the two amounts.
- Confirm your personal card is set as the form of payment.
- Acknowledge the booking contains a personal upgrade.
- Select Book flight to finalize your reservation.

You pay the full amount upfront and submit the policy-approved portion for reimbursement. The invoice/Itinerary you receive includes a Personal Upgrade Summary for your Expense Report.

Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.

# My expense report was rejected. What do I do now?

Most expense reports are processed and paid within 4 business days. If your submitted expense report has been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days, it's likely been flagged for additional review.

## **If your expense report is rejected:**

Rejected expense reports will be returned to you in the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) with guidance on how to fix and resubmit the expense report. You may be asked to submit legible receipts, provide additional justification for the expense, select a different expense type, or take other steps to fix the report.

Review the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) and if you believe your expense was rejected in error, share this feedback with a detailed justification in the comment field of the expense and resubmit the expense report.

## **If your expense report requires additional approvals: **

Reviewers such as your manager or skip-level manager will be added to your expense report. No action is needed from you at this stage unless you choose to follow up with the reviewer to provide additional context.

For assistance with resubmitting rejected expenses, contact the Amazon Global Expense Team through the [Expense & Corporate Cards RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).
# How long does it take to get reimbursed?

Most expense reports are processed and paid within 4 business days.

## Why your expense report may take longer to process:

If your submitted expense report has been pending with no movement for more than 3 business days, it's likely been flagged for additional review.

## **If your expense report is rejected:**

Reviewers such as your manager or skip-level manager will be added to your expense report. No action is needed from you at this stage unless you choose to follow up with the reviewer to provide additional context.

samples.query: Hey, since we're moving from Concur over to Emburse, if I accidentally swiped my corporate card for a personal charge, which expense tool's user guide should I follow to fix it and what's the exact step to mark the whole charge as personal so it gets deducted properly?
reference_contexts:
An expense tool migration is currently underway from [Concur](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) to [Emburse](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com).  Visit the Expense Tool Migration Hub to learn more about the migration timeline for your location.

Step-by-step guidance on how to submit an expense report can be found in the **How to Submit Expenses user guides **for [Concur](https://w.amazon.com/bin/Concur_Global_Expense/User_Guide) or [Emburse](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses)**.**
## **Repaying Personal Expenses (Emburse)**

If you accidentally used your corporate card for a personal expense, need to split an expense as partially personal, or otherwise need to repay Amazon, you will need to take the following steps to submit an expense report to reconcile the expense. See the [How to Submit Expenses user guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) for how to navigate the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com).

### **If you used your corporate card for personal use. **

Common Scenario: Hey, it happens, you accidentally swiped your corporate card instead of your personal card.

Find the corporate card charge in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet

If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense”.

Fill out the required fields.

Continue building your Expense Report and submit.

### **If you need to split an expense as partially personal.**

Common Scenario: You purchased multiple items in the same transaction but some of the expenses are ineligible for reimbursement per policy.

Find the corporate card charge in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet or create a new expense by uploading the receipt.

Apply the appropriate expense type as per the nature of the expense and fill out the required fields.

Under Allocation, click Add Allocation and select “PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense”

Adjust the amounts to reflect what amount is personal.

Continue building your Expense Report and submit.

### **If you were reimbursed for an expense in error and now need to pay back Amazon. **

Common Scenario: You created a corporate card expense from scratch instead of waiting for it to appear in Concur.  The system treats it as an out-of-pocket reimbursement and sends money to your personal account (as listed in Amazon Touchstone). Meanwhile, the actual corporate card charge remains "unsubmitted", which can lead to reminder emails - and even card suspension - if not corrected.

Find the corporate card charge in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet.

If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense”

Fill out the required fields and add a comment explaining your previous incorrect submission, including the original expense report number.

Continue building your Expense Report and submit.

## **What Happens Next**

When you submit expenses as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or the account you have set up in [Amazon Touchstone](https://touchstone.amazon.com/). Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension, so use your corporate card carefully.

For additional guidance visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).
samples.reference_answer: Even though an expense tool migration is currently underway from Concur to Emburse, the process for repaying an accidental personal charge on your corporate card is documented for Emburse, so you should follow the Emburse 'How to Submit Expenses' user guide (at atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) to navigate the Emburse Expense Tool. To mark the whole charge as personal: find the corporate card charge in the Expense Tool under your eWallet, select the Expense Type 'Personal Expense', fill out the required fields, then continue building and submit your Expense Report. Once submitted as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct that amount from future reimbursements or from the account set up in Amazon Touchstone.

# **What is the deadline for submitting expenses?**

The [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication) requires employees submit expense reports in the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) within 60 days of the transaction date. This applies to both out-of-pocket expenses (paid with cash or using your own credit card) and corporate card transactions (which will automatically populate in the [Expense Tool](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/) within 5 business days). If you fail to submit corporate card transactions within 60 days, your corporate card may be suspended.

## **Resubmitting if your expense report is rejected**

Most employee expense reports are processed and paid within 4 business days. If your expense report requires additional approvals or review, it could take longer than usual to be fully processed. Should you need to resubmit your expense report if it was initially rejected, the clock does not restart against the 60-day deadline.

samples.reference_answer: A team member's corporate card can be suspended for two related reasons drawn from the expense rules. reference_contexts:
# **I need to change my travel plans**

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to cancel or rebook your trip. Self-service supports cancellations and modifications for most bookings.

Refer to section 3.10 [Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **How to cancel or modify your flight **

- Navigate to your Upcoming Trips in the Travel section of your home page.
- If online self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.
- If further support is needed, visit the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for more information.
# **How do I cancel or modify a hotel booking?** (Global Access)

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to modify or cancel your hotel booking. Most hotel changes can be handled directly through self-service.

Refer to section 3.10 [Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Requirements before modifying**

Confirm your reservation meets all of the following requirements:

- You have not checked into the hotel.
- Your request falls within the cancellation policy guidelines.
- The hotel has not been paid for in advance.

If your reservation does not meet these requirements, contact [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

## **How to modify your hotel booking **

- Log in to the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).
- Navigate to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip.
- Find your hotel reservation and select Modify (or Cancel).
- Make your changes: Update check-in and check-out dates by editing the date fields and selecting Check Availability, or Select a different room type or rate.
- Review the changes and any price differences on the Checkout page.
- Select Book Hotel to confirm your modifications.

samples.query: If I accidentally charged a personal purchase to my corporate card in Emburse and want to pay Amazon back, how do I confirm that the 'Personal Expense' type I'm supposed to select is actually available to me, and where would I look it up?
reference_contexts:
# **How do I find the right expense type?**

The Expense Type Guide ([Concur](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/Expense_Type/), [Emburse](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide)) contains detailed descriptions of every expense type available for reimbursement in the Expense Tool ([Concur](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/), [Emburse](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com)), including country-specific restrictions.

If you don’t see the **expense type** you’re looking for in the Expense Tool ([Concur](https://expenses.corp.amazon.com/), [Emburse](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com)), then that expense isn’t available to you based on your location.
## **Repaying Personal Expenses (Emburse)**

If you accidentally used your corporate card for a personal expense, need to split an expense as partially personal, or otherwise need to repay Amazon, you will need to take the following steps to submit an expense report to reconcile the expense. See the [How to Submit Expenses user guide](https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/how-to-submit-expenses) for how to navigate the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com).

### **If you used your corporate card for personal use. **

Common Scenario: Hey, it happens, you accidentally swiped your corporate card instead of your personal card.

Find the corporate card charge in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet

If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense”.

Fill out the required fields.

Continue building your Expense Report and submit.

### **If you need to split an expense as partially personal.**

Common Scenario: You purchased multiple items in the same transaction but some of the expenses are ineligible for reimbursement per policy.

Find the corporate card charge in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet or create a new expense by uploading the receipt.

Apply the appropriate expense type as per the nature of the expense and fill out the required fields.

Under Allocation, click Add Allocation and select “PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense”

Adjust the amounts to reflect what amount is personal.

Continue building your Expense Report and submit.

### **If you were reimbursed for an expense in error and now need to pay back Amazon. **

Common Scenario: You created a corporate card expense from scratch instead of waiting for it to appear in Concur.  The system treats it as an out-of-pocket reimbursement and sends money to your personal account (as listed in Amazon Touchstone). Meanwhile, the actual corporate card charge remains "unsubmitted", which can lead to reminder emails - and even card suspension - if not corrected.

Find the corporate card charge in the [Expense Tool](https://app.us2.chromeriver.com/login/sso/saml?idp=amazon.com) under your eWallet.

If the entire amount is personal and non-reimbursable, select the Expense Type “Personal Expense”

Fill out the required fields and add a comment explaining your previous incorrect submission, including the original expense report number.

Continue building your Expense Report and submit.

## **What Happens Next**

When you submit expenses as personal, you authorize Amazon to deduct the amount from future reimbursements or the account you have set up in [Amazon Touchstone](https://touchstone.amazon.com/). Repeated personal use on a corporate card may result in card suspension, so use your corporate card carefully.

For additional guidance visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).
samples.reference_answer: To repay Amazon for a personal charge in Emburse, you locate the corporate card charge under your eWallet in the Expense Tool and select the Expense Type 'Personal Expense' (or, if only part is personal, add an allocation for 'PERSONAL Non-Reimbursable Personal Expense'), then complete the required fields and submit the report (per Document B). To confirm that the 'Personal Expense' type is actually available to you, you can check the Emburse Expense Type Guide (https://atoz.amazon.work/pages/amazon-travel-events-expense/expense-type-guide), which describes every reimbursable expense type and any country-specific restrictions. If the 'Personal Expense' type doesn't appear in the Expense Tool for you, then it isn't available based on your location (per Document A).

samples.query: If I need to adjust both my flight and my hotel for an upcoming trip through the self-service tool, what specific conditions would force me to go through a Travel Counselor instead, and is there a particular order I should follow when changing the hotel to avoid losing my reservation?
reference_contexts:
# **I need to change my travel plans**

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to cancel or rebook your trip. Self-service supports cancellations and modifications for most bookings.

Refer to section 3.10 [Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **How to cancel or modify your flight **

- Navigate to your Upcoming Trips in the Travel section of your home page.
- If online self-service changes are not available for your booking, contact a [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.
- If further support is needed, visit the [Amazon Travel & Events Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for more information.
# **How do I cancel or modify a hotel booking?** (Regional)

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to modify or cancel your hotel booking. Most hotel changes can be handled directly through self-service.

Refer to section 3.10 [Itinerary Changes and Cancellations](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-10) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Requirements before modifying**

Confirm your reservation meets all of the following requirements:

- You have not checked into the hotel.
- Your request falls within the cancellation policy guidelines.
- The hotel has not been paid for in advance.

If your reservation does not meet these requirements, contact [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

## **How to modify your hotel booking **

There are two types of hotel changes:

- Modify the dates of your current hotel reservation.
- Book an entirely new hotel (requires cancellation of the original reservation).

In both cases, book the new hotel or new dates first, then cancel the old reservation. This ensures you do not lose the current reservation if the preferred hotel is sold out.
samples.reference_answer: For the flight, you can use the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool for most changes, but if online self-service changes are not available for your booking, you must contact a Travel Counselor for assistance (Document A). For the hotel, you can also use self-service, but only if your reservation meets all requirements: you have not checked into the hotel, your request falls within the cancellation policy guidelines, and the hotel has not been paid for in advance. If your reservation does not meet these requirements, you must contact a Travel Counselor (Document B). When modifying the hotel—whether changing the dates or booking an entirely new hotel (which requires cancelling the original)—you should book the new hotel or new dates first, then cancel the old reservation, so you don't lose your current reservation if the preferred hotel is sold out (Document B). Both flight and hotel changes should also follow section 3.10 (Itinerary Changes and Cancellations) of the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

samples.query: earn personal miles business travel then upgrade with miles eligibility sweden booking tool
reference_contexts:
# **Can I upgrade my fare using miles?**

Yes. After booking travel through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/), you may contact the airline directly to request an upgrade using your personal loyalty miles. Mileage upgrades cannot be processed through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) — you must request them directly with your airline.

Refer to section 3.12 [Loyalty Programs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#c6c58a98-350b-aaa2-8c87-d9cb7f980da2) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Important limitations**

- Airline policies vary: Not all airlines allow upgrades with miles for tickets purchased through third-party booking platforms.
- Fare class restrictions: Basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically ineligible for mileage upgrades.
- Verification required: Review your specific fare rules and restrictions before requesting an upgrade.
# **Can I earn personal miles or loyalty points when traveling for business?**

Yes. You can earn personal loyalty miles and points when traveling for business through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/). This applies to airlines, hotels, and rental cars.

Refer to section 3.12 [Loyalty Programs](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#3-12) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Eligibility restrictions**

Employees based in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance requirements.

## **How to earn miles**

- Locate more information on the Amazon Travel & Events [Preferred Travel Providers and Promotions Wiki](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/BusinessTravelPromotions/).
- Sign up for loyalty programs.
- Add your loyalty program numbers to your Travel Profile.
- Miles and rewards are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip.

## **Important requirements**

- Ensure your loyalty numbers are accurate in your Travel Profile to avoid missing credits.
- Your Travel Profile name and your loyalty program name must match exactly.
- Contact the travel provider directly if miles are not credited after your trip.
samples.reference_answer: Earning and using personal loyalty miles are governed by section 3.12 (Loyalty Programs) of the Global Travel and Expense Policy. When you book through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool, you can earn personal loyalty miles/points on airlines, hotels, and rental cars, which are automatically credited to your personal account after each trip (you must add accurate loyalty numbers matching your Travel Profile name). However, employees based in Sweden, Norway, Denmark, and Israel are not eligible to participate in supplier point programs due to payroll tax compliance, so they cannot accrue miles to later use. For those who can earn miles, mileage upgrades cannot be processed through the Self-Service Travel Booking Tool — you must contact the airline directly to request an upgrade, and upgrades may be limited by airline policy and fare class (e.g., basic economy and deeply discounted fares are typically ineligible).

If your reservation does not meet these requirements, contact [Travel Counselor](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/) for assistance.

## **How to modify your hotel booking **

- Log in to the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).
- Navigate to Trips > Upcoming and select your trip.
- Find your hotel reservation and select Modify (or Cancel).
- Make your changes: Update check-in and check-out dates by editing the date fields and selecting Check Availability, or Select a different room type or rate.
- Review the changes and any price differences on the Checkout page.
- Select Book Hotel to confirm your modifications.

samples.reference_answer: Both flight and hotel changes are made in the same Self-Service Travel Booking Tool (booktravel.corp.amazon.com) under Trips > Upcoming, where you select your trip and choose Modify or Cancel for each booking, per section 3.10 (Itinerary Changes and Cancellations) of the Global Travel and Expense Policy. If you cancelled a hotel booking through the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) and were still charged, contact Amazon Travel Support to investigate.

Verify the following information before contacting [Amazon Travel Support](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/TravelAndEvents/Support/):

- Date and time of cancellation

# **How do I cancel or modify a hotel booking?** (Regional)

Use the [Self-Service Travel Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/) to modify or cancel your hotel booking. Most hotel changes can be handled directly through self-service.

## **Requirements before modifying**

Confirm your reservation meets all of the following requirements:

There are two types of hotel changes:

- Modify the dates of your current hotel reservation.

samples.query: I'm a frequent traveler covering edge cases for my team - if I've got a Switzerland-based employee versus a yellow-badge contractor traveling for us, which specific supplementary policy does each need to consult on top of the usual one, and does a manager or PXT sign-off let either of them bypass it?
reference_contexts:
# **What expenses are reimbursable?**

Amazon defines business expenses as those approved and necessary to the performance of your job, reflecting good judgment and [Amazon's Leadership Principle of Frugality](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/LeadershipPrinciples/Frugality/).

Consult the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) before making a purchase to confirm the expense is eligible for reimbursement. Manager or PXT approval does not constitute an acceptable exception to policy.

## **Switzerland-Specific Policy**

Employees based in Switzerland must also consult the [Switzerland Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/703733/publication) in addition to the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c).
# **What expenses are reimbursable?**

Amazon defines business expenses as those approved and necessary to the performance of your job, reflecting good judgment and [Amazon's Leadership Principle of Frugality](https://w.amazon.com/bin/view/LeadershipPrinciples/Frugality/).

Consult the [Global Travel and Expense Policy](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#76c58a98-34c4-136c-3e99-4d007e22b32c) before making a purchase to confirm the expense is eligible for reimbursement. Manager or PXT approval does not constitute an acceptable exception to policy.

## **Non-Amazon Employee Policy**

Travel and reimbursement expectations for non-Amazonians — including yellow badges (contract workers), green badges (temporary workers), candidates, or guests traveling on behalf of Amazon — are covered in the [Amazon Non-Employee Travel and Expense Policy.](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/610963/publication)

For further assistance, visit the [Employee Expense & Corporate Card RIVER](https://river.amazon.com/?org=EER).
samples.reference_answer: Both still start with the Global Travel and Expense Policy, but each has an additional policy to consult depending on their category. A Switzerland-based employee must also consult the Switzerland Travel and Expense Policy in addition to the Global Travel and Expense Policy (Document A). A yellow-badge contract worker (a non-Amazonian) is instead covered by the Amazon Non-Employee Travel and Expense Policy (Document B). In neither case does manager or PXT approval constitute an acceptable exception to policy - that sign-off cannot be used to bypass the applicable rules for either person.

samples.query: first time booking flight economy upgrade business class who do i tell manager approval
reference_contexts:
# **Am I allowed to upgrade my fare class?** (Regional)

Yes. Amazon reimburses only the Lowest Logical Fare in economy class. You must pay the difference between the policy-approved fare and the upgraded class out of pocket.

Refer to section 4.1[Airline Cabin Class](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#4-1) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **How to upgrade**

- Contact a Travel Counselor and request a personal upgrade for your trip.
- Pay for the entire ticket cost upfront using your own payment method.
- Obtain documentation showing the price difference between the Lowest Logical Fare and the upgraded class.
- When you expense your trip, claim reimbursement only for the documented Lowest Logical Fare noted by the travel services provider.

Upgrade fees and premium seating fees are not reimbursable.

## **Manager notification**

Your manager receives an email notification indicating you have added a personal upgrade. No manager action is required.
# **What flights am I allowed to book?**

Book the Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class that includes a seat assignment through the [Self-Service Booking Tool](https://booktravel.corp.amazon.com/).

Refer to section 4.1 [Airline Cabin Class](https://policy.a2z.com/docs/176324/publication#dec58a98-34c2-2f68-ea13-80ffad035827) in the Global Travel and Expense Policy.

## **Non-stop flight preference**

You may prioritize non-stop flights over connecting flights whenever possible and practical.

## **Exception for non-stop flights**

If a non-stop flight is not the Lowest Logical Fare, consult with your manager to determine the most appropriate flight option, balancing frugality and well-being.
samples.reference_answer: When booking your flight, you must use the Self-Service Booking Tool to book the Lowest Logical Fare in Coach/Economy Class with a seat assignment (Document B). If you want to upgrade your fare class, Amazon only reimburses the Lowest Logical Fare in economy, so you'd contact a Travel Counselor to request a personal upgrade, pay for the entire ticket upfront yourself, obtain documentation of the price difference, and then expense only the documented Lowest Logical Fare—upgrade and premium seating fees are not reimbursable (Document A). For the upgrade itself, no manager approval is required; your manager only receives an email notification that you added a personal upgrade and does not need to take any action (Document A). The only time you'd actually consult your manager about flights is a separate situation—when a non-stop flight is not the Lowest Logical Fare and you need to decide the most appropriate option balancing frugality and well-being (Document B).

- Expense the additional cost separately.
